title: A Goat Led Deputies on a 20-Minute Chase
script: One goat. Twenty minutes. Chaos! Tuesday afternoon, a loose goat started weaving through traffic near Pacific Lutheran University in Washington state — dodging cars like it had somewhere to be. Deputies and animal control raced in, but the goat had other plans, leading them on a full sprint through yards and parking lots. One deputy tried speaking what he called goat language to calm it down. The goat was not impressed. It took nearly twenty minutes before officers finally cornered the runaway on an outdoor staircase, boxed in with nowhere left to run. It's now resting at a local rescue farm while officials try to track down its owner. So who loses a goat and doesn't notice?
